extraction.one_line_summary: PORTE Richie won the 23.5km time trial by 12 seconds over MARTIN Tony, taking 37 seconds from FROOME Chris and seizing race leadership ambitions.
tactical_patterns: PORTE Richie gained decisive advantage in second half of TT after being only slightly ahead at mid-race checkpoint against FROOME.
VALVERDE Alejandro lost only 1 second per kilometer to PORTE, revealing unusual TT strength for late-season stage races.
CONTADOR Alberto sustained only 24 seconds deficit to MARTIN Tony at mid-race but collapsed in second half, exposing form vulnerability.
BARDET Romain lost 2 minutes total, showing rare time trial weakness that rivals can exploit in future mountain stages.

rider_signals.PORTE Richie: positive: Beat FROOME by 37 seconds and MARTIN by 12, demonstrating evolved TT capacity after focused winter training.
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: negative: Lost 10 seconds by mid-race and continued bleeding time in second half, suggesting compromised form compared to romandy.
rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: Third place finish with consistent pacing reveals he's carrying exceptional TT form into mountains despite racing continuously.
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: negative: Lost over 30 seconds per checkpoint to PORTE, exposing exploitable weakness in discipline ahead of mountain showdowns.
rider_signals.CONTADOR Alberto: negative: Competitive at mid-race but collapsed in second half, indicating insufficient sustained power for stage race ambitions.
rider_signals.MARTIN Tony: neutral: Set benchmark 50kph average but beaten by PORTE's second-half surge, confirming pure TT specialists vulnerable to climbing engines.
